description_long: This bungalow consists of several interconnected rectangular volumes, with a mix of flat and slightly sloped roofs. The exterior features a combination of light-colored plaster or concrete and sections of reddish-brown brickwork, giving it a modern yet grounded appearance. Large windows are distributed across the facades, some grouped and others spaced apart, providing ample natural light. The structure appears to be single-story, with a clear separation between different living areas, possibly including a garage or utility space. There are visible steps and entryways, but no prominent porch or veranda. The overall design is functional and contemporary, suitable for a suburban or urban neighborhood.
description: A modern single-story bungalow with flat and slightly sloped roofs, featuring a mix of light plaster and reddish-brown brick exteriors, and large, varied windows.
description_view_0: This view highlights the main living areas with flat roofs, visible window groupings, and a clear separation between the brick and plaster sections.
description_view_1: From this angle, the stepped entryway and the rear sections of the bungalow are visible, along with the arrangement of windows and the flat roof lines.
description_view_2: This perspective emphasizes the brickwork on the exterior walls and the flat roof, showing the rear and side facades with grouped windows.
description_view_3: This view shows the side and back of the bungalow, with the light-colored plaster sections and a clear view of the window placements and roof overhangs.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical with grouped and spaced windows
